response: Yes, both Arizona v. United States and Reynolds v. United States are Supreme Court cases. Arizona v. United States, 567 U.S. ___ (2012), involves the legality of a state law related to immigration enforcement, while Reynolds v. United States, 98 U.S. (8 Otto.) 145 (1878), addressed the constitutionality of religious duty as a defense in a criminal indictment. Both cases are significant in U.S. legal history and have been cited in subsequent Supreme Court decisions.
